issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
subject: Subscription pause
response: Thanks for reaching out. Good news — HackerRank has a **Pause Subscription** feature that lets you temporarily pause your subscription instead of canceling it, which sounds like what you need. To pause your subscription, log in and click your profile icon in the top-right corner, then select **Settings**. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**, and click the **Cancel Plan** button. You'll be prompted to choose a pause duration between 1 month and 12 months. Confirm your selection, and you'll receive a confirmation message with your automatic resume date. A couple of requirements to note: your subscription must have been active for at least 30 days, and you must be on a monthly plan (Individual Monthly, Basic, or Interview Monthly). When you're ready to resume, you can log in during the paused period and click **Resume Early** to access your billing page, where you can click **Resume Plan** and confirm. If you run into any issues with the pause process or have questions about your specific plan, I'd recommend contacting HackerRank support directly for hands-on assistance.
justification: Replied via LLM grounded on HackerRank snippets ('Pause Subscription', 'Pause Subscription'). Classified as product_issue / billing_and_payments, risk=low.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response: Thank you for your interest in setting up Claude LTI for your students. The documentation I have covers how to set up the Claude LTI integration in Canvas LMS, which involves two main parts: creating a developer key in Canvas and enabling the integration in Claude for Education. On the Canvas side, you'll need to sign in as an administrator, create an LTI developer key with specific settings (including the redirect URI and target link URI of https://claude.ai/lti/launch), and then install Claude LTI as an app using the Client ID. On the Claude side, you'll sign in to Claude for Education as an administrator, navigate to Organization settings > Connectors, enable Canvas, and input your Canvas Domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID. However, the snippets I have don't cover whether you already have a Claude for Education plan set up or what steps are needed to establish one in the first place. The documentation recommends contacting your university's administrator(s) if you have questions about your Claude for Education plan account or the Claude LTI setup. I'd recommend reaching out to Claude support directly for guidance on getting your institution started with the program.
justification: Replied via LLM grounded on Claude snippets ('Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure', 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure'). Classified as product_issue / general_help, risk=low.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response: Based on the documentation provided, what you're experiencing is actually permitted by Visa's rules. According to Visa's policy, merchants are generally not allowed to set minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions. However, there is a specific exception that applies to your situation: in US territories like the US Virgin Islands (as well as Puerto Rico and Guam), merchants may require a minimum transaction amount of $10 — but only for credit cards. So if your Visa card is a credit card, the $10 minimum the merchant is requesting is legally allowed in the US Virgin Islands. If your card is a debit card, the merchant should not be imposing this requirement. If you believe the merchant is incorrectly applying a minimum to a debit card, or if you have other concerns about this transaction, the documentation suggests you can fill out a form to report the issue to Visa. You should also notify your Visa card issuer (your bank) about the problem. I don't have the specific form link or contact details in the available documentation, so please reach out to your card issuer directly or contact Visa's support team for next steps.
justification: Replied via LLM grounded on Visa snippets ('Visa Consumer Support', 'Visa Travel Services | Visa Travel Credit Card Support'). Classified as product_issue / card_transactions, risk=low.
